content.text: 我开始写解析脚本。它会固定只吃两组 trace：`log-20260626-current-20260626-124131` 和 `log-20260626-forest-mimo-sglang-exp1`，先把你要的可直接观测指标稳定产出，再把无法可靠判断的项明确标成 `unknown`。

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628/build.ts
+import fs from "fs/promises"
+import path from "path"
+
+const ROOT = "/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628"
+const TRACE_DIRS = [
+  "/data/home/sheshuchen/ssc/dataset-trace/log-20260626-current-20260626-124131",
+  "/data/home/sheshuchen/ssc/dataset-trace/log-20260626-forest-mimo-sglang-exp1",
+]
+
+type TraceEvent = {
+  time: string
+  trace_id: string
+  event: string
+  method?: string
+  path?: string
+  status?: number
+  headers?: Record<string, string>
+  body?: any
+  chunk?: string
+  duration_ms?: number
+}
+
+type RequestRow = {
+  run_id: string
+  trace_dir: string
+  request_id: string
+  sequence_index: number
+  request_time: string
+  method: string
+  path: string
+  model: string
+  status: number | null
+  has_tools: boolean
+  num_tools_declared: number
+  tool_choice: string
+  message_count: number
+  system_chars: number
+  user_chars: number
+  assistant_chars: number
+  raw_request_bytes: number
+  messages_json_bytes: number
+  tools_json_bytes: number
+  prompt_tokens: number | null
+  completion_tokens: number | null
+  total_tokens: number | null
+  stream_chunk_count: number
+  time_to_first_chunk_ms: number | null
+  time_to_first_tool_call_ms: number | null
+  time_to_last_chunk_ms: number | null
+  actual_tool_calls: number
+  actual_tool_names: string
+  actual_tool_categories: string
+  finish_reasons: string
+  prefill_new_tokens_approx: number | null
+  prefill_change_reason: string
+}
+
+type ToolCallRow = {
+  run_id: string
+  trace_dir: string
+  request_id: string
+  sequence_index: number
+  tool_raw_name: string
+  tool_category: string
+  position_within_request: number
+}
+
+type RunRow = {
+  run_id: string
+  trace_dir: string
+  num_requests: number
+  num_streams: number
+  num_response_json: number
+  has_trace_jsonl: boolean
+  earliest_request_time: string
+  latest_request_time: string
+  count_pairing_ok: boolean
+  raw_turn_count: number
+  solved_label: string
+  p50_prompt_tokens: number | null
+  p90_prompt_tokens: number | null
+  max_prompt_tokens: number | null
+  p50_request_bytes: number | null
+  p90_request_bytes: number | null
+  max_request_bytes: number | null
+  total_actual_tool_calls: number
+  unique_actual_tool_names: number
+}
+
+function toCsv<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(rows: T[]): string {
+  if (rows.length === 0) return ""
+  const headers = Object.keys(rows[0])
+  const escape = (value: unknown) => {
+    if (value === null || value === undefined) return ""
+    const text = String(value)
+    if (/[",\n]/.test(text)) return `"${text.replaceAll('"', '""')}"`
+    return text
+  }
+  return [headers.join(","), ...rows.map((row) => headers.map((header) => escape(row[header])).join(","))].join("\n")
+}
+
+function percentile(values: number[], p: number): number | null {
+  if (values.length === 0) return null
+  const sorted = [...values].sort((a, b) => a - b)
+  const idx = Math.min(sorted.length - 1, Math.max(0, Math.ceil((p / 100) * sorted.length) - 1))
+  return sorted[idx]
+}
+
+function fileTimestampBase(name: string): string {
+  return name.replace(/-(request|response)\.json$|-stream\.txt$/, "")
+}
+
+function parseToolCategory(name: string): string {
+  const n = name.toLowerCase()
+  if (n === "task" || n.startsWith("task_")) return "task_like"
+  if (n === "memory" || n.startsWith("memory_")) return "memory_like"
+  if (n === "actor" || n.includes("subagent")) return "actor_like"
+  if (n.includes("read")) return "read_like"
+  if (n.includes("grep") || n.includes("glob") || n.includes("search") || n.includes("find")) return "search_like"
+  if (n.includes("edit") || n.includes("patch")) return "edit_like"
+  if (n === "write" || n.includes("write_")) return "write_like"
+  if (n.includes("bash") || n.includes("shell") || n.includes("command") || n.includes("exec")) return "shell_like"
+  return "other"
+}
+
+function extractMessageRoleChars(messages: any[], role: string): number {
+  return messages
+    .filter((msg) => msg?.role === role)
+    .map((msg) => {
+      if (typeof msg.content === "string") return msg.content.length
+      if (Array.isArray(msg.content)) return JSON.stringify(msg.content).length
+      return JSON.stringify(msg.content ?? "").length
+    })
+    .reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0)
+}
+
+function safeJsonSize(value: unknown): number {
+  return Buffer.byteLength(JSON.stringify(value ?? null), "utf8")
+}
+
+function parseSseMessages(raw: string): any[] {
+  return raw
+    .split("\n\n")
+    .map((block) => block.trim())
+    .filter((block) => block.startsWith("data: "))
+    .map((block) => block.slice(6))
+    .filter((payload) => payload && payload !== "[DONE]")
+    .flatMap((payload) => {
+      try {
+        return [JSON.parse(payload)]
+      } catch {
+        return []
+      }
+    })
+}
+
+async function main() {
+  await fs.mkdir(ROOT, { recursive: true })
+
+  const requestRows: RequestRow[] = []
+  const toolCallRows: ToolCallRow[] = []
+  const runRows: RunRow[] = []
+  const manifest: any[] = []
+
+  for (const traceDir of TRACE_DIRS) {
+    const runID = path.basename(traceDir)
+    const entries = await fs.readdir(traceDir)
+    const requestFiles = entries.filter((x) => x.endsWith("-request.json")).sort()
+    const streamFiles = entries.filter((x) => x.endsWith("-stream.txt")).sort()
+    const responseFiles = entries.filter((x) => x.endsWith("-response.json")).sort()
+    const hasTrace = entries.includes("trace.jsonl")
+    const traceEvents = hasTrace
+      ? (await fs.readFile(path.join(traceDir, "trace.jsonl"), "utf8"))
+          .split("\n")
+          .filter((line) => line.trim())
+          .flatMap((line) => {
+            try {
+              return [JSON.parse(line) as TraceEvent]
+            } catch {
+              return []
+            }
+          })
+      : []
+    const byTraceID = new Map<string, TraceEvent[]>()
+    for (const event of traceEvents) {
+      const list = byTraceID.get(event.trace_id) ?? []
+      list.push(event)
+      byTraceID.set(event.trace_id, list)
+    }
+
+    manifest.push({
+      run_id: runID,
+      trace_dir: traceDir,
+      num_request_json: requestFiles.length,
+      num_stream_txt: streamFiles.length,
+      num_response_json: responseFiles.length,
+      has_trace_jsonl: hasTrace,
+      earliest_file_prefix: requestFiles[0] ? fileTimestampBase(requestFiles[0]) : "",
+      latest_file_prefix: requestFiles.at(-1) ? fileTimestampBase(requestFiles.at(-1)!) : "",
+      pairing_ok: requestFiles.length === streamFiles.length + responseFiles.length,
+    })
+
+    let prevSerialized = ""
+    let prevPromptTokens: number | null = null
+    let prevTools = ""
+    let prevMessageCount = 0
+
+    for (const [index, requestFile] of requestFiles.entries()) {
+      const requestPath = path.join(traceDir, requestFile)
+      const requestRaw = await fs.readFile(requestPath, "utf8")
+      const requestJson = JSON.parse(requestRaw)
+      const traceID = requestJson.trace_id as string
+      const body = requestJson.body ?? {}
+      const messages = Array.isArray(body.messages) ? body.messages : []
+      const tools = Array.isArray(body.tools) ? body.tools : []
+      const responsePath = path.join(traceDir, `${traceID}-response.json`)
+      const streamPath = path.join(traceDir, `${traceID}-stream.txt`)
+      const hasResponse = await fs
+        .access(responsePath)
+        .then(() => true)
+        .catch(() => false)
+      const hasStream = await fs
+        .access(streamPath)
+        .then(() => true)
+        .catch(() => false)
+      const responseJson = hasResponse ? JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(responsePath, "utf8")) : null
+      const events = (byTraceID.get(traceID) ?? []).sort((a, b) => a.time.localeCompare(b.time))
+      const requestEvent = events.find((e) => e.event === "request")
+      const responseHeaderEvent = events.find((e) => e.event === "response_headers")
+      const streamEvents = events.filter((e) => e.event === "stream_chunk")
+      const streamRaw = hasStream ? await fs.readFile(streamPath, "utf8") : ""
+      const sseMessages = parseSseMessages(streamRaw)
+
+      const toolNameParts = new Map<number, string>()
+      const toolNamesOrdered: string[] = []
+      const finishReasons = new Set<string>()
+      let usage: { prompt_tokens?: number; completion_tokens?: number; total_tokens?: number } | null = null
+      let firstToolCallTime: string | null = null
+
+      for (const streamEvent of streamEvents) {
+        const payloads = parseSseMessages(streamEvent.chunk ?? "")
+        for (const payload of payloads) {
+          if (payload?.usage) usage = payload.usage
+          const choices = Array.isArray(payload?.choices) ? payload.choices : []
+          for (const choice of choices) {
+            if (choice?.finish_reason) finishReasons.add(String(choice.finish_reason))
+            const toolCalls = Array.isArray(choice?.delta?.tool_calls) ? choice.delta.tool_calls : []
+            if (toolCalls.length > 0 && !firstToolCallTime) firstToolCallTime = streamEvent.time
+            for (const toolCall of toolCalls) {
+              const idx = typeof toolCall?.index === "number" ? toolCall.index : toolNamesOrdered.length
+              const current = toolNameParts.get(idx) ?? ""
+              const next = current + (toolCall?.function?.name ?? "")
+              toolNameParts.set(idx, next)
+            }
+          }
+        }
+      }
+
+      const sortedToolNames = [...toolNameParts.entries()]
+        .sort((a, b) => a[0] - b[0])
+        .map(([, name]) => name)
+        .filter((name) => name.length > 0)
+      for (const [pos, name] of sortedToolNames.entries()) {
+        toolNamesOrdered.push(name)
+        toolCallRows.push({
+          run_id: runID,
+          trace_dir: traceDir,
+          request_id: traceID,
+          sequence_index: index + 1,
+          tool_raw_name: name,
+          tool_category: parseToolCategory(name),
+          position_within_request: pos + 1,
+        })
+      }
+
+      const currentSerialized = JSON.stringify({
+        messages: body.messages ?? [],
+        tools: body.tools ?? [],
+        tool_choice: body.tool_choice ?? null,
+      })
+      let sharedChars = 0
+      const minLen = Math.min(prevSerialized.length, currentSerialized.length)
+      while (sharedChars < minLen && prevSerialized.charCodeAt(sharedChars) === currentSerialized.charCodeAt(sharedChars)) {
+        sharedChars += 1
+      }
+      const currentPromptTokens = typeof usage?.prompt_tokens === "number" ? usage.prompt_tokens : null
+      const prefillApprox =
+        currentPromptTokens && currentSerialized.length > 0
+          ? Math.max(0, Math.round(currentPromptTokens * (1 - sharedChars / currentSerialized.length)))
+          : null
+      const currentTools = JSON.stringify(body.tools ?? [])
+      const prefillReason =
+        index === 0
+          ? "first_request"
+          : currentTools !== prevTools
+            ? "tools_changed"
+            : messages.length !== prevMessageCount
+              ? "message_count_changed"
+              : sharedChars < currentSerialized.length * 0.5
+                ? "large_prompt_change"
+                : "small_append_or_edit"
+
+      const requestTime = requestEvent?.time ?? ""
+      const firstChunkTime = streamEvents[0]?.time ?? null
+      const lastChunkTime = streamEvents.at(-1)?.time ?? null
+      const baseMs = requestTime ? new Date(requestTime).getTime() : null
+      const diffMs = (time: string | null) => (time && baseMs !== null ? new Date(time).getTime() - baseMs : null)
+
+      requestRows.push({
+        run_id: runID,
+        trace_dir: traceDir,
+        request_id: traceID,
+        sequence_index: index + 1,
+        request_time: requestTime,
+        method: requestJson.method ?? "",
+        path: requestJson.path ?? "",
+        model: body.model ?? "",
+        status: responseHeaderEvent?.status ?? responseJson?.status ?? null,
+        has_tools: tools.length > 0,
+        num_tools_declared: tools.length,
+        tool_choice: typeof body.tool_choice === "string" ? body.tool_choice : JSON.stringify(body.tool_choice ?? null),
+        message_count: messages.length,
+        system_chars: extractMessageRoleChars(messages, "system"),
+        user_chars: extractMessageRoleChars(messages, "user"),
+        assistant_chars: extractMessageRoleChars(messages, "assistant"),
+        raw_request_bytes: Buffer.byteLength(requestRaw, "utf8"),
+        messages_json_bytes: safeJsonSize(messages),
+        tools_json_bytes: safeJsonSize(tools),
+        prompt_tokens: currentPromptTokens,
+        completion_tokens: typeof usage?.completion_tokens === "number" ? usage.completion_tokens : null,
+        total_tokens: typeof usage?.total_tokens === "number" ? usage.total_tokens : null,
+        stream_chunk_count: sseMessages.length,
+        time_to_first_chunk_ms: diffMs(firstChunkTime),
+        time_to_first_tool_call_ms: diffMs(firstToolCallTime),
+        time_to_last_chunk_ms: diffMs(lastChunkTime),
+        actual_tool_calls: sortedToolNames.length,
+        actual_tool_names: sortedToolNames.join("|"),
+        actual_tool_categories: [...new Set(sortedToolNames.map(parseToolCategory))].join("|"),
+        finish_reasons: [...finishReasons].join("|"),
+        prefill_new_tokens_approx: prefillApprox,
+        prefill_change_reason: prefillReason,
+      })
+
+      prevSerialized = currentSerialized
+      prevPromptTokens = currentPromptTokens ?? prevPromptTokens
+      prevTools = currentTools
+      prevMessageCount = messages.length
+    }
+
+    const runRequestRows = requestRows.filter((row) => row.run_id === runID)
+    const promptTokens = runRequestRows.flatMap((row) => (row.prompt_tokens !== null ? [row.prompt_tokens] : []))
+    const requestBytes = runRequestRows.map((row) => row.raw_request_bytes)
+    const uniqueToolNames = new Set(
+      toolCallRows.filter((row) => row.run_id === runID).map((row) => row.tool_raw_name),
+    )
+    runRows.push({
+      run_id: runID,
+      trace_dir: traceDir,
+      num_requests: requestFiles.length,
+      num_streams: streamFiles.length,
+      num_response_json: responseFiles.length,
+      has_trace_jsonl: hasTrace,
+      earliest_request_time: runRequestRows[0]?.request_time ?? "",
+      latest_request_time: runRequestRows.at(-1)?.request_time ?? "",
+      count_pairing_ok: requestFiles.length === streamFiles.length + responseFiles.length,
+      raw_turn_count: requestFiles.length,
+      solved_label: "unknown",
+      p50_prompt_tokens: percentile(promptTokens, 50),
+      p90_prompt_tokens: percentile(promptTokens, 90),
+      max_prompt_tokens: promptTokens.length ? Math.max(...promptTokens) : null,
+      p50_request_bytes: percentile(requestBytes, 50),
+      p90_request_bytes: percentile(requestBytes, 90),
+      max_request_bytes: requestBytes.length ? Math.max(...requestBytes) : null,
+      total_actual_tool_calls: toolCallRows.filter((row) => row.run_id === runID).length,
+      unique_actual_tool_names: uniqueToolNames.size,
+    })
+  }
+
+  const summary = {
+    generated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
+    trace_dirs: TRACE_DIRS,
+    run_count: runRows.length,
+    request_count: requestRows.length,
+    tool_call_count: toolCallRows.length,
+    top_tool_categories: Object.entries(
+      toolCallRows.reduce<Record<string, number>>((acc, row) => {
+        acc[row.tool_category] = (acc[row.tool_category] ?? 0) + 1
+        return acc
+      }, {}),
+    )
+      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
+      .map(([name, count]) => ({ name, count })),
+    top_tool_names: Object.entries(
+      toolCallRows.reduce<Record<string, number>>((acc, row) => {
+        acc[row.tool_raw_name] = (acc[row.tool_raw_name] ?? 0) + 1
+        return acc
+      }, {}),
+    )
+      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
+      .slice(0, 20)
+      .map(([name, count]) => ({ name, count })),
+    prefill_reason_counts: Object.entries(
+      requestRows.reduce<Record<string, number>>((acc, row) => {
+        acc[row.prefill_change_reason] = (acc[row.prefill_change_reason] ?? 0) + 1
+        return acc
+      }, {}),
+    )
+      .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
+      .map(([name, count]) => ({ name, count })),
+  }
+
+  await fs.writeFile(path.join(ROOT, "trace_manifest.json"), JSON.stringify(manifest, null, 2))
+  await fs.writeFile(path.join(ROOT, "runs_table.csv"), toCsv(runRows))
+  await fs.writeFile(path.join(ROOT, "requests_table.csv"), toCsv(requestRows))
+  await fs.writeFile(path.join(ROOT, "tool_calls_table.csv"), toCsv(toolCallRows))
+  await fs.writeFile(path.join(ROOT, "summary.json"), JSON.stringify(summary, null, 2))
+}
+
+await main()
*** End Patch

{"cmd":"python - <<'PY'
import csv, statistics, math
from collections import Counter,defaultdict
rows=list(csv.DictReader(open('research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628/requests_table.csv', encoding='utf-8')))
for run_id in sorted(set(r['run_id'] for r in rows)):
    sub=[r for r in rows if r['run_id']==run_id]
    def nums(key):
        out=[]
        for r in sub:
            v=r[key]
            if v not in ('', None): out.append(float(v))
        return out
    def pct(arr,p):
        if not arr: return None
        arr=sorted(arr)
        idx=max(0,min(len(arr)-1, math.ceil(p/100*len(arr))-1))
        return arr[idx]
    prompt=nums('prompt_tokens')
    reqb=nums('raw_request_bytes')
    first=nums('time_to_first_chunk_ms')
    tool=nums('actual_tool_calls')
    prefill=nums('prefill_new_tokens_approx')
    print('RUN',run_id)
    print(' requests',len(sub),'has_tools',sum(r['has_tools']=='true' for r in sub),'tool_requests',sum(float(r['actual_tool_calls'])>0 for r in sub))
    print(' prompt_tokens p50/p90/max', pct(prompt,50), pct(prompt,90), max(prompt) if prompt else None)
    print(' request_bytes p50/p90/max', pct(reqb,50), pct(reqb,90), max(reqb) if reqb else None)
    print(' ttfc_ms p50/p90/max', pct(first,50), pct(first,90), max(first) if first else None)
    print(' actual_tool_calls p50/p90/max', pct(tool,50), pct(tool,90), max(tool) if tool else None)
    print(' prefill_new_tokens_approx p50/p90/max', pct(prefill,50), pct(prefill,90), max(prefill) if prefill else None)
    print(' status', Counter(r['status'] or 'missing' for r in sub))
    print(' reasons', Counter(r['prefill_change_reason'] for r in sub))
    print()
PY","workdir":"/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code","max_output_tokens":6000}

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628/研究执行记录-20260628.md
+# Agent Trace 指标调研执行记录（2026-06-28）
+
+## 1. 任务目标
+
+本次执行的目标是按 [research_plans/2026-06-28-agent-trace-metrics-plan.md](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_plans/2026-06-28-agent-trace-metrics-plan.md:1) 的顺序，优先完成“从简单到难”的前几层指标抽取，并把结果落成可复跑的脚本和结构化表格。
+
+关注的问题包括：
+
+- agent 工作中有哪些工具调用种类；
+- 工具调用在请求中的出现情况和粗粒度时间分布；
+- request 长度量级；
+- 近似的 prefill 新增 token 数分布；
+- 轮次数量与可用的 run 级统计。
+
+## 2. 本次实际执行范围
+
+这次没有强行一次做完整个 9 阶段计划，而是优先完成了**可直接从现有 trace 稳定观测**的部分：
+
+1. Trace inventory
+2. Canonical request/run tables
+3. Request length scale
+4. Tool-call category inventory
+5. Raw turn count
+6. Coarse timing
+7. Approximate prefill new-token distribution
+
+暂未可靠完成的部分：
+
+- `solved / failed` 精确标签
+- `turns-to-solve`
+- refined prefill new-token 分析
+- 跨成功/失败的相关性分析
+
+原因不是脚本没写，而是这两组 trace 本身缺少足够稳定的 outcome 标注和后端 cache 真值信号。
+
+## 3. 输入数据
+
+本次固定只分析两组 trace：
+
+1. `/data/home/sheshuchen/ssc/dataset-trace/log-20260626-current-20260626-124131`
+2. `/data/home/sheshuchen/ssc/dataset-trace/log-20260626-forest-mimo-sglang-exp1`
+
+选择理由：
+
+- 一组有完整 `trace.jsonl`，适合做 timing 与事件还原；
+- 一组没有完整 `trace.jsonl`，能逼出 parser 的 fallback 路径；
+- 两组都包含 `request.json` / `stream.txt`，可覆盖主分析流程。
+
+## 4. 新增产物
+
+### 4.1 解析脚本
+

+### 6.2 `forest` trace
+
+- request 数：21
+- `prompt_tokens`：

+- `forest` 这组 prompt token 甚至更长，p90 已达到 `35k+`。
+- 说明它非常适合后续继续做“前缀复用 / prefill 新增 token / reminder 注入”的深挖。

+## 7. 工具调用种类
+
+### 7.1 全局 top categories
+

+1. `bash`

+## 10. Approximate Prefill New Tokens
+
+### 10.1 近似方法
+
+本次用的是**近似法**，不是后端 cache 真值：

+
+- 强启发式

+
+### 10.2 `current` trace
+
+- `prefill_new_tokens_approx`
+  - p50 = 16819
+  - p90 = 26530
+  - max = 33667
+

+
+- `tools_changed`: 49
+- `message_count_changed`: 11
+- `first_request`: 1
+
+### 10.3 `forest` trace
+
+- `prefill_new_tokens_approx`
+  - p50 = 17071
+  - p90 = 30981
+  - max = 32133
+

+
+- `message_count_changed`: 15
+- `large_prompt_change`: 3
+- `tools_changed`: 2
+- `first_request`: 1

+

+
+这与两个目录的使用风格是相符的：
+
+- `current` 更偏动态工具/agent loop
+- `forest` 更偏长会话和上下文生长
+
+## 11. Outcome / Solve 分析为什么没有继续做
+
+计划里后面的 `solved_label`、`turns-to-solve`、跨成功/失败相关性，这次没有硬做，原因很具体：
+
+1. 这两组 trace 没有稳定的 benchmark outcome 文件和统一完成标志。
+2. 单看 stream 文本，很难不靠人工主观判断就定义 solve turn。
+3. `forest` 组没有完整时间事件，也让后续 cross-metric join 变弱。
+
+因此当前 `runs_table.csv` 中：
+
+- `solved_label = unknown`
+
+这是刻意保守，不是漏做。
+
+## 12. 本次完成了哪些计划阶段
+
+### 已完成
+
+- Task 1: Trace inventory
+- Task 2: Minimal canonical tables
+- Task 3: Request length scale
+- Task 4: Tool-call category inventory
+- Task 5: Raw turn count（未推进到 turns-to-solve）
+- Task 6: Coarse timing（仅 `current` trace）
+- Task 7: Approximate prefill new-token distribution
+
+### 未完成
+
+- Task 5 的 outcome label / turns-to-solve
+- Task 8 refined prefill
+- Task 9 final correlation report
+
+## 13. 下一步建议
+
+如果继续往后做，我建议严格按下面顺序：
+
+1. 先补一组**天然带 outcome label** 的 corpus  
+   例如 SWE-bench / mini-swe-agent 的成功与失败运行目录。
+
+2. 给 parser 增加 `run_outcomes.csv` 的外部输入  
+   不再从 stream 文本中猜 solved/failed。
+
+3. 再做 refined prefill  
+   特别是把以下结构变化显式标注出来：
+   - tool list change
+   - checkpoint rebuild
+   - reminder injection
+   - user message append
+
+4. 最后做 correlation  
+   否则会出现“长度很长 / tool call 很多 / 但 outcome 还是 unknown”的尴尬局面。
+
+## 14. 一句话总结
+
+这次执行已经把**最基础、最稳的观测层**跑通了：  
+两组 trace 共 82 个 request，241 次实际工具调用，主导工具是 `bash` / `read`，请求长度普遍在 `20k+ prompt tokens`，并且近似 prefill 新增 token 也普遍处在高位；但要继续做“解决任务轮次”和“成功/失败相关性”，必须先补稳定的 outcome 标签数据源。
*** End Patch

两组 trace 共 82 个 request，241 次实际工具调用，主导工具是 `bash` / `read`，请求长度普遍在 `20k+ prompt tokens`，并且近似 prefill 新增 token 也普遍处在高位；但要继续做“解决任务轮次”和“成功/失败相关性”，必须先补稳定的 outcome 标签数据源。

### 3.4 和当前 MiMoCode 研究目标的关系

如果目标是“研究 agent 推理链路、cache/session/program 级调度、复杂工具调用 prompt 对模型的影响”，`MiMoCode` 更接近真实上游。  
如果目标是“先拿一个最小 agent 跑通 SWE-bench benchmark”，`Mini-SWE-Agent` 更直接。

## 4. 本次做了什么，按时间顺序列

## 4.1 新建独立 SWE-bench 仓库并安装

最初在 `agent-trace-proxy/logs` 下新建独立 clone，后面移动到了 `~/SWE-bench`。

执行过的关键命令：

```bash
git clone https://github.com/SWE-bench/SWE-bench.git SWE-bench
cd SWE-bench
python3 -m venv .venv
.venv/bin/pip install -e .
.venv/bin/pip install -e '.[test]'
.venv/bin/pip install socksio
```

目的：

- 独立 clone，不碰原项目环境。
- 所有依赖放在 `SWE-bench/.venv`。
- `socksio` 用来解决本机 SOCKS 代理环境下的 `httpx` 依赖缺失。

## 4.2 验证 SWE-bench 官方 CLI

执行：

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m swebench.harness.run_evaluation --help
```

结果：

- 通过。
- 证明 `SWE-bench` 安装和 CLI 入口正常。

## 4.3 验证 SWE-bench 非 Docker 测试

执行：

```bash
PATH=.venv/bin:$PATH .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_cli.py::test_smoke_test tests/test_harness_utils.py -q
```

结果：

```text
6 passed
```

说明：

- 这里主要验证 Python 包装层和非容器逻辑。
- 不等于完整 benchmark 可以运行。

## 4.4 验证 SWE-bench 官方 gold 单实例

执行：

```bash
.venv/bin/python -m swebench.harness.run_evaluation \
  --predictions_path gold \
  --max_workers 1 \
  --instance_ids sympy__sympy-20590 \
  --run_id validate-gold
```

先后暴露了两个问题：

### 问题 A：`socksio` 缺失

报错核心：

```text
ImportError: Using SOCKS proxy, but the 'socksio' package is not installed.
```

处理：

```bash
.venv/bin/pip install socksio
```

### 问题 B：Docker socket 权限

继续执行后，数据集加载通过，最后报错：

```text
docker.errors.DockerException: Error while fetching server API version:
('Connection aborted.', PermissionError(13, 'Permission denied'))
```

这说明：

- Hugging Face 数据集链路已经通了；
- 真正失败在 Docker daemon。

Output:
# SWE-bench 非 Docker 最小测试记录

## 目标

在不使用 Docker 的前提下，验证 `Mini-SWE-Agent + vLLM` 是否能对 `SWE-Bench Lite/dev` 的单条实例真正启动起来，并定位最小阻断点。

本次测试日期：`2026-06-25`

## 结论

结论只有三点：

1. `Mini-SWE-Agent` 的 SWE-bench 流程可以绕过 Docker，改用 `local environment` 在宿主机目录直接运行。
2. 这条非 Docker 路径已经成功走到：
   - 加载 `SWE-Bench Lite` 数据集
   - 过滤到单个实例 `sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625`
   - 在本地目录检出该实例对应仓库和 commit
   - 构造首轮 prompt
   - 向本地 `vLLM` 发起首轮 `chat/completions` 请求
3. 当前最小卡点不是 Docker，也不是本地 Git 检出，而是模型上下文窗口太小：
   - 使用的模型是 `Qwen3-1.7B`
   - `vLLM` 服务启动参数里 `--max-model-len 1024`
   - `Mini-SWE-Agent` 的首轮 SWE-bench prompt 已接近或超过这个上限
   - 因此单实例在第一轮推理前就因 `ContextLengthExceeded` 退出

换句话说，这次已经证明“非 Docker 能跑起来”，但没有跑过首轮 agent 推理，原因是模型上下文长度，不是环境机制本身。

## 测试对象

- SWE-bench 子集：`princeton-nlp/SWE-Bench_Lite`
- split：`dev`
- 单实例：`sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625`
- 对应仓库：`sqlfluff/sqlfluff`
- 对应 commit：`14e1a23a3166b9a645a16de96f694c77a5d4abb7`
- Agent：`Mini-SWE-Agent 1.14.4`
- 模型后端：本地 `vLLM`
- 模型：`Qwen3-1.7B`

## 为什么这次不需要 Docker

SWE-bench 默认依赖容器，是为了把每个实例固定在独立代码版本、依赖和测试环境中，保证 benchmark 可复现。

但 `Mini-SWE-Agent` 自己支持多种 environment class，不只有 `docker`。代码里可选项包括：

- `docker`
- `singularity`
- `local`
- `bubblewrap`

这次选择的是 `local`，意思是：

- 不启动实例容器
- 直接在宿主机的某个工作目录执行命令
- 由 `env_startup_command` 负责把目标仓库检出到该目录

因此它不是“假装没用 Docker”，而是明确走了 `Mini-SWE-Agent` 自带的非 Docker 执行路径。

## 关键配置

为了最小化验证链路，新增了本地配置文件：

- `/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/agent-trace-proxy/logs/mini-swebench-no-docker-local.yaml`

配置的关键点：

- `environment_class: local`
- `cwd: /tmp/testbed`
- `run.env_startup_command` 使用 Git 在本地检出目标仓库和目标 commit
- `model_class: vllm`
- `model_name: Qwen3-1.7B`
- `max_completion_tokens` 最后压到 `128`
- `step_limit: 2`

本地检出命令是：

```bash
git init . &&
(git remote get-url origin >/dev/null 2>&1 && git remote remove origin || true) &&
git remote add origin https://github.com/{{ repo }}.git &&
git fetch --depth 1 origin {{ base_commit }} &&
git checkout --detach FETCH_HEAD
```

这里专门把 `origin` 删除后重建，是因为第一次复跑时发现 `/tmp/testbed` 里的旧 remote 会让启动命令失败。

## 实际执行命令

### 1. 启动本地 vLLM

```bash
PATH=/data/home/sheshuchen/venvs/vllm-official/bin:$PATH \
CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=3 \
VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL=INFO \
/data/home/sheshuchen/venvs/vllm-official/bin/python \
  -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \
  --model /data1/models/Qwen3-1.7B \
  --served-model-name Qwen3-1.7B \
  --host 127.0.0.1 \
  --port 8100 \
  --dtype bfloat16 \
  --max-model-len 1024 \
  --gpu-memory-utilization 0.08 \
  --enforce-eager
```

### 2. 验证 vLLM 接口已就绪

```bash
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8100/v1/models
```

返回 `200`，说明本地 OpenAI 兼容接口可用。

### 3. 运行非 Docker 的单实例 SWE-bench 烟测

```bash
PATH=/data/home/sheshuchen/vllm-continuum/.venv/bin:$PATH \
mini-extra swebench \
  --config /data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/agent-trace-proxy/logs/mini-swebench-no-docker-local.yaml \
  --subset lite \
  --split dev \
  --filter '^sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625$' \
  --workers 1 \
  --output /tmp/mini-swebench-no-docker-local-4
```

## 过程中遇到的卡点

### 卡点 1：vLLM 启动时找不到 `ninja`

第一次起 `vLLM` 时，进程在 FlashInfer JIT 阶段报错：

```text
FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: 'ninja'
```

原因不是 `ninja` 包没装，而是启动命令没有把 venv 的 `bin` 目录放进 `PATH`。

修正方式：

- 在启动 `vLLM` 时显式加上
  `PATH=/data/home/sheshuchen/venvs/vllm-official/bin:$PATH`

修正后，`vLLM` 服务成功启动。

### 卡点 2：`Mini-SWE-Agent` 默认 `max_completion_tokens=2048`

第一次非 Docker 单实例运行时，还没真正进入 agent 执行，就被 `vLLM` 拒绝：

```text
max_completion_tokens=2048 cannot be greater than max_model_len=max_total_tokens=1024
```

原因：

- 这份 `mini-swe-agent` 的 `VllmModelConfig` 默认 `max_completion_tokens` 是 `2048`
- 而当前 `vLLM` 服务用的是 `--max-model-len 1024`

修正方式：

- 在本地配置里显式把 `max_completion_tokens` 下调
- 先试 `256`
- 后试 `128`

### 卡点 3：本地工作目录复用导致 `git remote origin already exists`

第二次运行时，错误已经推进到本地 repo 初始化阶段：

```text
Reinitialized existing Git repository in /tmp/testbed/.git/
error: remote origin already exists.
```

说明这时已经不在 Docker 逻辑里，而是在执行 `local environment` 的启动命令。

修正方式：

- 启动命令改成幂等
- 先删除已有的 `origin`，再重新添加

### 卡点 4：模型上下文仍然不够

最后一次运行已经完成了：

- 数据集加载
- 单实例过滤
- 本地仓库检出
- trajectory 保存
- 首轮模型请求发送

但仍在首轮请求时失败，最终退出状态是 `ContextLengthExceeded`。

关键报错：

```text
This model's maximum context length is 1024 tokens.
However, you requested 128 output tokens and your prompt contains at least 897 input tokens,
for a total of at least 1025 tokens.
```

这说明：

- 这条实例的首轮系统提示词 + 任务描述 + 规则约束已经非常长
- 即使把输出 token 压到 `128`
- 对 `1024` 上下文窗口的 `Qwen3-1.7B` 仍然不够

## 这个结果说明了什么

这个结果可以严格拆成两部分：

### 已经验证成功的部分

- 非 Docker 执行路径可用
- `Mini-SWE-Agent` 能用 `local environment` 代替 `docker`
- `env_startup_command` 能把目标实例仓库检出到宿主机目录
- `Mini-SWE-Agent` 能基于该目录准备单实例运行
- 本地 `vLLM` 能接收到来自 SWE-bench agent 的真实请求

### 仍未完成的部分

Chunk ID: 1ae481
Wall time: 0.0000 seconds
Process exited with code 0
Original token count: 591
Output:
---

## 6. 运行命令

```bash
cd ~/ssc/dataset-trace/swebench-local-20260626-attempt2

PYTHONPATH=/data/home/sheshuchen/mini-swe-agent/src \
MSWEA_COST_TRACKING=ignore_errors \
/data/home/sheshuchen/vllm-continuum/.venv/bin/python3 \
  -m minisweagent.run.benchmarks.swebench_single \
  --subset lite --split test \
  -i astropy__astropy-12907 \
  -c swebench-local-config-30b.yaml \
  --environment-class local \
  -y --exit-immediately \
  -o astropy__astropy-12907.traj.json \
  2>&1 | tee agent-run.log
```

---

## 7. 运行结果

**最终状态**：`LimitsExceeded`（40 步耗尽）  
**Trajectory 消息数**：83（含系统、用户、assistant、tool_result）  
**总 token 消耗**：约 2.5M tokens（提示 token 因 prefix cache 命中大幅复用）  
**运行时长**：约 60 分钟（4.7 tokens/s × 平均 500 tokens/步 × 40 步）  

---

## 8. Agent 行为分析

### 8.1 步骤分解

| 阶段 | 步骤 | 行为 |
|------|------|------|
| 探索期 | 1–4 | 正确找到 `separable.py`，读取 `separability_matrix`、`_separable`、`_cstack` 三个函数 |
| 循环期 | 5–40 | 在 `grep -A 50 -B 50 "def _cstack"` / `def _separable` / `def _coord_matrix` 之间来回循环，未写任何修复 |

### 8.2 关键发现

1. **模型找到了正确位置**：steps 1-4 中，模型正确定位了 `_cstack` 函数，这比 Qwen3-1.7B（完全没有探索源码）有质的提升。

2. **content: null 现象**：所有 assistant 消息均为 `"content": null`（只有 `tool_calls` 字段），这是因为配置了 `enable_thinking: false`，导致 Qwen3 模型完全不输出文字推理，仅输出工具调用。

3. **没有 THOUGHT 过程**：instance_template 要求模型在每步输出 THOUGHT 推理，但 thinking 被禁用后模型无法产生文字内容，只能连续发出 tool_call。这使得模型无法积累推理状态，每步独立调用 grep，在同一组函数间无休止循环。

4. **推理能力 vs 工具调用格式**：禁用 thinking 虽然节省了 token，却破坏了 Qwen3 的核心推理路径。对于需要深度理解代码并规划修复方案的任务，chain-of-thought 是必须的。

### 8.3 失败原因总结

| 原因 | 影响 |
|------|------|
| `enable_thinking: false` | 模型无法在步与步之间积累推理状态 |
| 40 步限制 | 循环期占用了全部 36 步 |

content.text: 我没理解若我启动mini-swe-agent,那swe bench的作用或者角色是什么

payload.message: 我没理解若我启动mini-swe-agent,那swe bench的作用或者角色是什么

SWE-bench 是**题库 + 任务规范 + 评测环境**，mini-swe-agent 是**做题的 agent**。

可以把它理解成：

- `SWE-bench`：出题老师  
  它提供每道题的：
  - issue / PR 描述
  - 对应仓库
  - 对应 commit
  - 评测方式
  - 有时还负责把代码检出到指定环境里

- `mini-swe-agent`：答题的人  
  它做的事是：
  - 读取这道题
  - 读代码、跑命令、改代码
  - 产出 patch / trajectory
  - 尝试把题解出来

所以当你启动 `mini-swe-agent` 跑 SWE-bench 时，SWE-bench 的角色不是 agent，而是 **benchmark substrate**。

更具体一点，mini-swe-agent 跑 SWE-bench 时，通常会借用 SWE-bench 提供的几样东西：

1. 数据集  
   例如 `SWE-Bench_Lite` 里的某个实例，如 `astropy__astropy-12907`。

2. 实例元数据  
   包括：
   - 目标 repo
   - base commit
   - 题目描述
   - 可能的测试上下文

3. 任务边界  
   告诉 agent：你是在解哪一个 issue，对哪个代码版本动手。

4. 评测/复现框架  
   后续如果你要严格算“解没解出来”，还是要回到 SWE-bench 的 harness 或它兼容的评测逻辑。

所以一句话：

**你启动的是 mini-swe-agent 在做题；SWE-bench 提供的是题目、代码版本和评测规则。**

不是：

- mini-swe-agent 和 SWE-bench 二选一
- 也不是 mini-swe-agent 替代了 SWE-bench

而是：

- `SWE-bench = benchmark`
- `mini-swe-agent = solver`

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接画一个最清楚的链路：

`SWE-bench instance -> mini-swe-agent -> local/vllm model -> patch/trajectory -> SWE-bench evaluation`

content.text: 我还是不太了解，测试数据从哪里拿到的？swe-bench的仓库吗？测评怎么实现的？和大模型有关系吗？还是一个字一个字对照diff？

payload.message: 我还是不太了解，测试数据从哪里拿到的？swe-bench的仓库吗？测评怎么实现的？和大模型有关系吗？还是一个字一个字对照diff？

对，**测试数据首先就是从 SWE-bench 这套数据里来的**，通常来源于它对应的数据集和仓库工具链，而不只是“看一个 GitHub 仓库 README”。

你可以把整件事拆成 4 层：

## 1. 测试数据从哪里来

SWE-bench 的“题目”本质上是一个个实例，每个实例通常包含：

- 一个 issue / PR 描述
- 一个目标代码仓库名
- 一个 base commit
- 相关测试信息或可验证行为

所以数据来源是：

- **SWE-bench 数据集本身**
- 再加上它指向的**目标开源仓库历史版本**

也就是说，不是单独从 `SWE-bench` 仓库源码里“抄题”，而是：

- SWE-bench 数据集告诉你“测哪道题”
- 然后把对应 repo checkout 到指定 commit
- 再让 agent 在那个代码版本上修

这就是为什么你会看到像：

- `sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625`
- `astropy__astropy-12907`

这种实例 ID。

## 2. mini-swe-agent 在里面做什么

mini-swe-agent 不负责“定义题目”，它负责“解题”：

- 读取某个 SWE-bench instance
- 把对应 repo 检出到本地目录
- 把题目描述塞进 prompt
- 让大模型读代码、调用工具、改代码
- 产出 patch / trajectory

所以它像“考生”。

## 3. 测评怎么实现

**不是简单按 diff 一字一句比对。**

核心不是：

- 你的 patch 和官方 patch 完全一样才算对

而是更接近：

- 把你改完的代码放回这个 repo 环境里
- 跑指定测试 / 相关测试
- 看问题有没有真的被修掉，同时别把别的东西搞坏

也就是说，SWE-bench 更关注**行为正确性**，不是 patch 文本完全一致。

因为同一个 bug 往往有很多合法修法：

- 你写的 diff 可能和官方 PR 不同
- 但只要测试和行为满足要求，也可能算成功

## 4. 和大模型的关系是什么

关系很大，但位置要分清：

- **SWE-bench**：负责出题和验题
- **大模型**：负责想办法修题
- **mini-swe-agent**：负责把大模型接到真实代码环境和工具上

所以大模型不是 evaluator，**大模型是 solver**。

评测流程通常是：

1. 从 SWE-bench 取一个实例
2. checkout 对应 repo + commit
3. agent 驱动大模型改代码
4. 生成 patch
5. 用评测逻辑跑测试
6. 根据测试结果判断是否解决

所以最终判分更偏：

- “你修完后程序是否通过验证”

而不是：

- “你写的 diff 是否长得和参考答案完全一样”

## 最后用一句最短的话总结

**SWE-bench 提供题目和验题规则，mini-swe-agent + 大模型负责做题，最终通常靠运行代码/测试来判，不是逐字对照 diff。**

如果你要，我下一条可以专门给你画一张非常具体的流程图，例如：

`SWE-bench instance -> checkout repo@commit -> mini-swe-agent + LLM -> patch -> test harness -> pass/fail`

对，**测试数据首先就是从 SWE-bench 这套数据里来的**，通常来源于它对应的数据集和仓库工具链，而不只是“看一个 GitHub 仓库 README”。

你可以把整件事拆成 4 层：

## 1. 测试数据从哪里来

SWE-bench 的“题目”本质上是一个个实例，每个实例通常包含：

- 一个 issue / PR 描述
- 一个目标代码仓库名
- 一个 base commit
- 相关测试信息或可验证行为

所以数据来源是：

- **SWE-bench 数据集本身**
- 再加上它指向的**目标开源仓库历史版本**

也就是说，不是单独从 `SWE-bench` 仓库源码里“抄题”，而是：

- SWE-bench 数据集告诉你“测哪道题”
- 然后把对应 repo checkout 到指定 commit
- 再让 agent 在那个代码版本上修

这就是为什么你会看到像：

- `sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625`
- `astropy__astropy-12907`

这种实例 ID。

## 2. mini-swe-agent 在里面做什么

mini-swe-agent 不负责“定义题目”，它负责“解题”：

- 读取某个 SWE-bench instance
- 把对应 repo 检出到本地目录
- 把题目描述塞进 prompt
- 让大模型读代码、调用工具、改代码
- 产出 patch / trajectory

所以它像“考生”。

## 3. 测评怎么实现

**不是简单按 diff 一字一句比对。**

核心不是：

- 你的 patch 和官方 patch 完全一样才算对

而是更接近：

- 把你改完的代码放回这个 repo 环境里
- 跑指定测试 / 相关测试
- 看问题有没有真的被修掉，同时别把别的东西搞坏

也就是说，SWE-bench 更关注**行为正确性**，不是 patch 文本完全一致。

因为同一个 bug 往往有很多合法修法：

- 你写的 diff 可能和官方 PR 不同
- 但只要测试和行为满足要求，也可能算成功

## 4. 和大模型的关系是什么

关系很大，但位置要分清：

- **SWE-bench**：负责出题和验题
- **大模型**：负责想办法修题
- **mini-swe-agent**：负责把大模型接到真实代码环境和工具上

所以大模型不是 evaluator，**大模型是 solver**。

评测流程通常是：

1. 从 SWE-bench 取一个实例
2. checkout 对应 repo + commit
3. agent 驱动大模型改代码
4. 生成 patch
5. 用评测逻辑跑测试
6. 根据测试结果判断是否解决

所以最终判分更偏：

- “你修完后程序是否通过验证”

而不是：

- “你写的 diff 是否长得和参考答案完全一样”

## 最后用一句最短的话总结

**SWE-bench 提供题目和验题规则，mini-swe-agent + 大模型负责做题，最终通常靠运行代码/测试来判，不是逐字对照 diff。**

如果你要，我下一条可以专门给你画一张非常具体的流程图，例如：

`SWE-bench instance -> checkout repo@commit -> mini-swe-agent + LLM -> patch -> test harness -> pass/fail`

settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

import { Cause, Deferred, Effect, Layer, Context, Scope } from "effect"
import * as Stream from "effect/Stream"
import { Agent } from "@/agent/agent"
import { SYSTEM_SPAWNED_AGENT_TYPES } from "@/agent/config"
import { Bus } from "@/bus"
import { Metrics } from "@/metrics"
import { Config } from "@/config"
import { Permission } from "@/permission"
import { Plugin } from "@/plugin"
import { Snapshot } from "@/snapshot"
import * as Session from "./session"
import { LLM } from "./llm"
import { MessageV2 } from "./message-v2"
import { isOverflow } from "./overflow"
import { PartID } from "./schema"
import type { SessionID } from "./schema"
import { SessionRetry } from "./retry"
import { SessionStatus } from "./status"
import { SessionSummary } from "./summary"
import type { Provider } from "@/provider"
import { Question } from "@/question"
import { errorMessage } from "@/util/error"
import { isRecoverableError } from "@/tool/recoverable"
import { Log } from "@/util"
import { isRecord } from "@/util/record"

const DOOM_LOOP_THRESHOLD = 3
const log = Log.create({ service: "session.processor" })

export type Result = "overflow" | "stop" | "continue"

export type Event = LLM.Event

/**
 * A proposed tool call captured from a candidate stream (max mode), before
 * any execution. `input` is the parsed tool arguments.
 */
export type ProposedToolCall = {
  toolCallId: string
  toolName: string
  input: Record<string, any>
  providerMetadata?: Record<string, any>
}

/**
 * The winning candidate selected by the judge in max mode. Captures the full
 * reasoning + text + proposed tool calls of one candidate stream so the
 * processor can replay it: lay down the reasoning/text parts and actually
 * execute the tool calls via the execute-bearing tools.
 */
export type ReplayInput = {
  reasoning?: string
  reasoningMetadata?: Record<string, any>
  text?: string
  textMetadata?: Record<string, any>
  toolCalls: ProposedToolCall[]
  finishReason: string
  usage?: any
  providerMetadata?: Record<string, any>
  /** Execute-bearing tools (from resolveTools) used to run the winner's calls. */
  tools: Record<string, { execute?: (input: any, options: any) => Promise<any> }>
  /** Model messages passed to tool execute contexts. */
  messages: any[]
  /**
   * Max-mode selection metadata. When set, a short note is prepended to the
   * replayed reasoning so the user can see the ensemble happened and which
   * candidate won. `winner` is 0-based.
   */
  selection?: { winner: number; total: number }
  /**
   * Real wall-clock duration (ms) the winning candidate spent thinking, so the
   * replayed reasoning part shows a meaningful duration instead of the ~1ms
   * synthetic replay time. Optional.
   */
  thinkingMs?: number
  /**
   * Max-mode ensemble overhead: the cost and token counts of the losing
   * candidates + the judge call. These are real spend but consume NO context,
   * so they are added to `cost` and the ModelCall metric ONLY — never to the
   * message's `tokens`, which must stay the winner's real footprint so context
   * overflow / prune estimation stays correct.
   */
  overhead?: { cost: number; tokensIn: number; tokensOut: number }
}

export interface Handle {
  readonly message: MessageV2.Assistant
  readonly updateToolCall: (
    toolCallID: string,
    update: (part: MessageV2.ToolPart) => MessageV2.ToolPart,
  ) => Effect.Effect<MessageV2.ToolPart | undefined>
  readonly completeToolCall: (
    toolCallID: string,
    output: {
      title: string
      metadata: Record<string, any>
      output: string
      attachments?: MessageV2.FilePart[]
    },
  ) => Effect.Effect<void>
  readonly process: (streamInput: LLM.StreamInput) => Effect.Effect<Result>
  /**
   * Replay a pre-selected candidate (max mode): synthesize the stream events a
   * real model call would have produced for the winner — reasoning, text, then
   * each tool call (input-start → call → execute → result) — and run them
   * through the same event handler used by `process`. Tool execution, snapshot
   * tracking, permission asks and metrics are all reused. Returns the same
   * Result contract as `process`.
   */
  readonly replay: (input: ReplayInput) => Effect.Effect<Result>
}

export type AgentMetrics = {
  tokens_in: number
  tokens_out: number
  files_changed: number
}

type Input = {
  assistantMessage: MessageV2.Assistant
  sessionID: SessionID
  model: Provider.Model
  agentMetrics?: AgentMetrics
}

export interface Interface {
  readonly create: (input: Input) => Effect.Effect<Handle>
}

type ToolCall = {
  partID: MessageV2.ToolPart["id"]
  messageID: MessageV2.ToolPart["messageID"]
  sessionID: MessageV2.ToolPart["sessionID"]
  done: Deferred.Deferred<void>
}

interface ProcessorContext extends Input {
  toolcalls: Record<string, ToolCall>
  shouldBreak: boolean
  snapshot: string | undefined
  blocked: boolean
  needsOverflowHandling: boolean
  currentText: MessageV2.TextPart | undefined
  reasoningMap: Record<string, MessageV2.ReasoningPart>
  stepStartedAt: number | undefined
  firstTokenAt: number | undefined
  stepPartIds: PartID[]
}

type StreamEvent = Event

export class Service extends Context.Service<Service, Interface>()("@opencode/SessionProcessor") {}

export const layer: Layer.Layer<
  Service,
  never,
  | Session.Service
  | Config.Service
  | Bus.Service
  | Snapshot.Service
  | Agent.Service
  | LLM.Service
  | Permission.Service
  | Plugin.Service
  | SessionSummary.Service
  | SessionStatus.Service
> = Layer.effect(
  Service,
  Effect.gen(function* () {
    const session = yield* Session.Service
    const config = yield* Config.Service
    const bus = yield* Bus.Service

content.text: 查看MiMo-Code代码，说明除了prompt.ts文件之外，还有哪里有system reminder?比如checkpoint writer之类的？

有，而且 `checkpoint-writer` 这类最关键的并不在 `prompt.ts` 里。

主要位置是这些：

- `checkpoint-writer` 主提示是在 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:268) 里拼的。`composeWriterPrompt()` 直接包了一层 `<system-reminder>...You are now operating in checkpoint-writer mode...</system-reminder>`。真正的大段 writer 规则正文来自 [checkpoint-writer.txt](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/agent/prompt/checkpoint-writer.txt:1)，但外层 reminder 是 `checkpoint.ts` 加的。
- `checkpoint writer` 校验失败后的重试 reminder 在 [checkpoint-retry.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint-retry.ts:141)。`buildReflectionMessage()` 会生成 “The previous attempt at this checkpoint had validation errors...” 这类 `<system-reminder>`。
- 跟 checkpoint 同一文件里还有几个 loop 相关 reminder，不在 `prompt.ts`，在 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:50)：
  - `autonomousLoopReminder()`
  - `stopReminder()`
  - `toolResultContinueReminder()`

另外，除了 `prompt.ts` / checkpoint 系列，还有几类别的注入点：

- 未完成任务的 stop-gate reminder 在 [task/gate.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/task/gate.ts:42)。会生成 “You are about to finish, but these tasks...”
- `read` 工具会把加载到的附加指令包成 `<system-reminder>` 追加到读文件结果后面，在 [read.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/tool/read.ts:262)。这类常见来源就是 `AGENTS.md` / 其他 instructions 文件。
- 还有一些提示片段文件本身就写成了 `<system-reminder>`，例如：
  - [compose.txt](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt/compose.txt:1)
  - [build-switch.txt](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt/build-switch.txt:1)

补一个区分：

- [prompt.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:1838) 里更多是主 session loop 的 runtime reminder，比如 output-length continuation、goal not satisfied、invalid output、structured output retry、recall reminder、context fill、repeated-step、用户中途插话包装等。
- `checkpoint-writer` / checkpoint retry 这一支是另一套来源，核心在 `session/checkpoint.ts` 和 `session/checkpoint-retry.ts`，不是 `prompt.ts`。

如果你要，我可以下一步把这些 reminder 按“会不会跨后续轮次保留”再分成一张表。
在MiMoCode创建新的文件夹，叫做docs-explore,写中文文档总结你上面的发现，然后提交到远程的git@github.com:medicalfaculty/mimo-code.git

已在 [docs-explore/system-reminder-source-summary.md](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/docs-explore/system-reminder-source-summary.md:1) 新增中文总结，内容聚焦 `checkpoint-writer` 不在 `prompt.ts`、而是在 `session/checkpoint.ts` / `checkpoint-retry.ts`，并把其他 `system reminder` 来源按模块做了归类。

提交已创建并推送到你指定的远程 `git@github.com:medicalfaculty/mimo-code.git` 的 `ssc` 分支。提交是 `2e6e07c`，commit message 为 `docs: summarize system reminder sources`。这次没有跑测试，因为只新增了文档，没有改代码。
checkpoint是每次一轮对话存储检查点，还是多轮对话多个任务之后把这多个任务上下文发送给子agent保存检查点？是任务+reminder，还是对话1+对话2+对话3+...+reminder？

更接近第二种，但要精确一点：

**不是每轮对话都存一次 checkpoint。**  
它是在 **上下文 token 到达阈值时** 才触发 checkpoint writer，而不是一轮一轮写。阈值逻辑在 [prune.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/prune.ts:240)，默认是按上下文窗口百分比触发，比如中等窗口模型默认 `20%/40%/60%/80%`，大窗口更密。

所以它不是：

- 对话1就写一次
- 对话2再写一次
- 对话3再写一次

而是更像：

- 主 session 累积了多轮对话、多个步骤、可能也跨多个任务
- 到了 checkpoint threshold
- 才启动一个 `checkpoint-writer` 子 agent 去总结并写 `checkpoint.md`

核心触发点在 [prompt.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/prompt.ts:2463) 调 `fireCheckpoints()`，真正启动 writer 在 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:500)。

你问“它拿到的是 `任务+reminder`，还是 `对话1+对话2+对话3+...+reminder`？”

答案是：

**更接近 `对话历史片段 + reminder`，不是单纯 `任务+reminder`。**

具体分两种模式：

1. **默认 `fork: false`**
   - writer 继承的是 **自上一个 checkpoint 之后，到这次 watermark 为止的那一段主对话历史 delta**
   - 代码在 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:675)
   - `delta = msgs.slice(alignedStart, watermarkIdx + 1)`
   - 这里的 `msgs` 是主 agent 的消息切片，不是 task-only 数据

2. **如果配置 `checkpoint.fork: true`**
   - writer 会继承 **从会话开头到当前 watermark 的整段主 slice**
   - 代码在 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:725)
   - `msgsAtWatermark = msgs.slice(0, watermarkIdx + 1)`

所以：

- 默认不是“每轮单独存”
- 默认也不是“只发任务信息”
- 默认是“**上次 checkpoint 之后累计起来的多轮主对话上下文 + checkpoint-writer reminder/prompt**”

任务信息怎么进来，要分开看：

- **任务树状态**不是从历史对话里硬总结，而是 writer 再调用 `task` tool 现查，见 [checkpoint-writer.txt](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/agent/prompt/checkpoint-writer.txt:69)
- **subagent 的任务进展**会通过 `buildProgressDiff()` 形成一个 `SUBAGENT PROGRESS to integrate` 提示块塞进 writer prompt，见 [checkpoint-progress-reconcile.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint-progress-reconcile.ts:81) 和 [checkpoint.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/checkpoint.ts:615)
- writer 再按这个提示去读 `tasks/<id>/progress.md`

所以最准确的描述是：

**checkpoint writer 的输入 = 一段累计的主对话历史 + checkpoint-writer 自己的 reminder/prompt + 少量任务侧辅助信息。**

不是：

- 每轮一存
- 也不是纯 task snapshot

而是：

- **多轮累计后，按阈值触发**
- **把一段 conversation slice 交给子 agent**
- **再结合 task tool 和 progress diff 写 checkpoint**

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接给你画一个“主 agent -> threshold -> checkpoint-writer -> checkpoint.md”的时序图。

最简单分两层，看你要测什么。

如果你只是想确认 **SWE-bench 官方仓库本身能启动**，最小步骤是：

```bash

python3 -m venv .venv

PATH=.venv/bin:$PATH .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_cli.py::test_smoke_test tests/test_harness_utils.py -q
```

如果你想跑一个 **最小单实例 agent 测试**，你现在仓库里现成可参考的是 mini-swe-agent 这条链，最短命令在 [README.md](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/swebench_run_docs/dataset-trace/swebench-local-20260626-attempt2/README.md:120)：

```bash
cd /data/home/sheshuchen/ssc/dataset-trace/swebench-local-20260626-attempt2

PYTHONPATH=/data/home/sheshuchen/mini-swe-agent/src \
MSWEA_COST_TRACKING=ignore_errors \

  -m minisweagent.run.benchmarks.swebench_single \

```

如果你要的是“**不碰 agent，只验证 SWE-bench 官方单实例能跑到 harness**”，再加这一条：


- **测 SWE-bench 安装是否正常**：跑 `run_evaluation --help` 和那条 `pytest`

我没理解若我启动mini-swe-agent,那swe bench的作用或者角色是什么

SWE-bench 是**题库 + 任务规范 + 评测环境**，mini-swe-agent 是**做题的 agent**。

可以把它理解成：

- `SWE-bench`：出题老师  
  它提供每道题的：
  - issue / PR 描述
  - 对应仓库
  - 对应 commit
  - 评测方式
  - 有时还负责把代码检出到指定环境里

- `mini-swe-agent`：答题的人  
  它做的事是：
  - 读取这道题
  - 读代码、跑命令、改代码
  - 产出 patch / trajectory
  - 尝试把题解出来

所以当你启动 `mini-swe-agent` 跑 SWE-bench 时，SWE-bench 的角色不是 agent，而是 **benchmark substrate**。

更具体一点，mini-swe-agent 跑 SWE-bench 时，通常会借用 SWE-bench 提供的几样东西：

1. 数据集  
   例如 `SWE-Bench_Lite` 里的某个实例，如 `astropy__astropy-12907`。

2. 实例元数据  
   包括：
   - 目标 repo
   - base commit
   - 题目描述
   - 可能的测试上下文

3. 任务边界  
   告诉 agent：你是在解哪一个 issue，对哪个代码版本动手。

4. 评测/复现框架  
   后续如果你要严格算“解没解出来”，还是要回到 SWE-bench 的 harness 或它兼容的评测逻辑。

所以一句话：

**你启动的是 mini-swe-agent 在做题；SWE-bench 提供的是题目、代码版本和评测规则。**

不是：

- mini-swe-agent 和 SWE-bench 二选一
- 也不是 mini-swe-agent 替代了 SWE-bench

而是：

- `SWE-bench = benchmark`
- `mini-swe-agent = solver`

如果你愿意，我下一条可以直接画一个最清楚的链路：

`SWE-bench instance -> mini-swe-agent -> local/vllm model -> patch/trajectory -> SWE-bench evaluation`
我还是不太了解，测试数据从哪里拿到的？swe-bench的仓库吗？测评怎么实现的？和大模型有关系吗？还是一个字一个字对照diff？

对，**测试数据首先就是从 SWE-bench 这套数据里来的**，通常来源于它对应的数据集和仓库工具链，而不只是“看一个 GitHub 仓库 README”。

你可以把整件事拆成 4 层：

## 1. 测试数据从哪里来

SWE-bench 的“题目”本质上是一个个实例，每个实例通常包含：

- 一个 issue / PR 描述
- 一个目标代码仓库名
- 一个 base commit
- 相关测试信息或可验证行为

所以数据来源是：

- **SWE-bench 数据集本身**
- 再加上它指向的**目标开源仓库历史版本**

也就是说，不是单独从 `SWE-bench` 仓库源码里“抄题”，而是：

- SWE-bench 数据集告诉你“测哪道题”
- 然后把对应 repo checkout 到指定 commit
- 再让 agent 在那个代码版本上修

这就是为什么你会看到像：

- `sqlfluff__sqlfluff-1625`
- `astropy__astropy-12907`

这种实例 ID。

## 2. mini-swe-agent 在里面做什么

mini-swe-agent 不负责“定义题目”，它负责“解题”：

- 读取某个 SWE-bench instance
- 把对应 repo 检出到本地目录
- 把题目描述塞进 prompt
- 让大模型读代码、调用工具、改代码
- 产出 patch / trajectory

所以它像“考生”。

## 3. 测评怎么实现

**不是简单按 diff 一字一句比对。**

核心不是：

- 你的 patch 和官方 patch 完全一样才算对

而是更接近：

- 把你改完的代码放回这个 repo 环境里
- 跑指定测试 / 相关测试
- 看问题有没有真的被修掉，同时别把别的东西搞坏

也就是说，SWE-bench 更关注**行为正确性**，不是 patch 文本完全一致。

因为同一个 bug 往往有很多合法修法：

- 你写的 diff 可能和官方 PR 不同
- 但只要测试和行为满足要求，也可能算成功

## 4. 和大模型的关系是什么

关系很大，但位置要分清：

- **SWE-bench**：负责出题和验题
- **大模型**：负责想办法修题
- **mini-swe-agent**：负责把大模型接到真实代码环境和工具上

所以大模型不是 evaluator，**大模型是 solver**。

评测流程通常是：

1. 从 SWE-bench 取一个实例
2. checkout 对应 repo + commit
3. agent 驱动大模型改代码
4. 生成 patch
5. 用评测逻辑跑测试
6. 根据测试结果判断是否解决

所以最终判分更偏：

- “你修完后程序是否通过验证”

而不是：

- “你写的 diff 是否长得和参考答案完全一样”

## 最后用一句最短的话总结

**SWE-bench 提供题目和验题规则，mini-swe-agent + 大模型负责做题，最终通常靠运行代码/测试来判，不是逐字对照 diff。**

如果你要，我下一条可以专门给你画一张非常具体的流程图，例如：

`SWE-bench instance -> checkout repo@commit -> mini-swe-agent + LLM -> patch -> test harness -> pass/fail`

# AGENTS.md instructions for /data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code

<INSTRUCTIONS>
- Always use superpowers skill instead of builtin plan mode.
- To regenerate the JavaScript SDK, run `./packages/sdk/js/script/build.ts`.
- ALWAYS USE PARALLEL TOOLS WHEN APPLICABLE.
- The default branch in this repo is `dev`.
- Local `main` ref may not exist; use `dev` or `origin/dev` for diffs.
- Prefer automation: execute requested actions without confirmation unless blocked by missing info or safety/irreversibility.

## Style Guide

### General Principles

- Keep things in one function unless composable or reusable
- Avoid `try`/`catch` where possible
- Avoid using the `any` type
- Use Bun APIs when possible, like `Bun.file()`
- Rely on type inference when possible; avoid explicit type annotations or interfaces unless necessary for exports or clarity
- Prefer functional array methods (flatMap, filter, map) over for loops; use type guards on filter to maintain type inference downstream
- In `src/config`, follow the existing self-export pattern at the top of the file (for example `export * as ConfigAgent from "./agent"`) when adding a new config module.

Reduce total variable count by inlining when a value is only used once.

```ts
// Good
const journal = await Bun.file(path.join(dir, "journal.json")).json()

// Bad
const journalPath = path.join(dir, "journal.json")
const journal = await Bun.file(journalPath).json()
```

### Destructuring

Avoid unnecessary destructuring. Use dot notation to preserve context.

```ts
// Good
obj.a
obj.b

// Bad
const { a, b } = obj
```

### Variables

Prefer `const` over `let`. Use ternaries or early returns instead of reassignment.

```ts
// Good
const foo = condition ? 1 : 2

// Bad
let foo
if (condition) foo = 1
else foo = 2
```

### Control Flow

Avoid `else` statements. Prefer early returns.

```ts
// Good
function foo() {
  if (condition) return 1
  return 2
}

// Bad
function foo() {
  if (condition) return 1
  else return 2
}
```

### Schema Definitions (Drizzle)

Use snake_case for field names so column names don't need to be redefined as strings.

```ts
// Good
const table = sqliteTable("session", {
  id: text().primaryKey(),
  project_id: text().notNull(),
  created_at: integer().notNull(),
})

// Bad
const table = sqliteTable("session", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  projectID: text("project_id").notNull(),
  createdAt: integer("created_at").notNull(),
})
```

## Testing

- Avoid mocks as much as possible
- Test actual implementation, do not duplicate logic into tests
- Tests cannot run from repo root (guard: `do-not-run-tests-from-root`); run from package dirs like `packages/opencode`.

## Type Checking

- Always run `bun typecheck` from package directories (e.g., `packages/opencode`), never `tsc` directly.

</INSTRUCTIONS>

payload.message:
当前代码里，**没有一个统一、现成的“每次 tool 精确执行耗时”字段**可以直接看。你现在能稳定拿到的主要是两类：

1. **请求级耗时**
   在 `agent-trace-proxy` 产出的 `trace.jsonl` / `*-response.json` 里有 `duration_ms`，这是**整次模型请求**的耗时，不是某个 tool 自己跑了多久。我们现在的统计脚本也是基于这个做粗 timing，见 [build.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628/build.ts:1)。

2. **tool 出现的时间点**
   也就是“第一个 tool call 大概在什么时候出现”，这可以从 trace event 的时间戳近似推出，但它仍然不是“tool 从开始到结束的精确 duration”。这个限制我已经写在 [研究执行记录-20260628.md](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/research_execution/agent-trace-metrics-20260628/研究执行记录-20260628.md:1) 里了。

如果你问“MiMoCode 框架内部哪里处理 tool call”，入口在 [processor.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/session/processor.ts:1)。这里能看到 `updateToolCall` / `completeToolCall` 这类生命周期处理，但我没查到一个统一持久化的 `started_at` / `finished_at` / `duration_ms` 字段。

例外是**个别具体工具**自己会带 elapsed 信息，比如 ripgrep 解析里会读底层返回的 `elapsed`，见 [ripgrep.ts](/data/home/sheshuchen/MiMo-Code/packages/opencode/src/file/ripgrep.ts:1)。但这只是某个工具自己的数据，不是通用框架指标。

一句话结论：**现在默认能看的是“请求耗时”和“tool call 出现时间”，看不到统一的“每次 tool 执行了多少毫秒”；如果你要这个指标，需要在 tool 执行前后自己加埋点。**

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.

---
name: brainstorming
description: "You MUST use this before any creative work - creating features, building components, adding functionality, or modifying behavior. Explores user intent, requirements and design before implementation."
---

# Brainstorming Ideas Into Designs

Help turn ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

Start by understanding the current project context, then ask questions one at a time to refine the idea. Once you understand what you're building, present the design and get user approval.

<HARD-GATE>
Do NOT invoke any implementation skill, write any code, scaffold any project, or take any implementation action until you have presented a design and the user has approved it. This applies to EVERY project regardless of perceived simplicity.
</HARD-GATE>

## Anti-Pattern: "This Is Too Simple To Need A Design"

Every project goes through this process. A todo list, a single-function utility, a config change — all of them. "Simple" projects are where unexamined assumptions cause the most wasted work. The design can be short (a few sentences for truly simple projects), but you MUST present it and get approval.

## Checklist

You MUST create a task for each of these items and complete them in order:

1. **Explore project context** — check files, docs, recent commits
2. **Offer visual companion** (if topic will involve visual questions) — this is its own message, not combined with a clarifying question. See the Visual Companion section below.
3. **Ask clarifying questions** — one at a time, understand purpose/constraints/success criteria
4. **Propose 2-3 approaches** — with trade-offs and your recommendation
5. **Present design** — in sections scaled to their complexity, get user approval after each section
6. **Write design doc** — save to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md` and commit
7. **Spec self-review** — quick inline check for placeholders, contradictions, ambiguity, scope (see below)
8. **User reviews written spec** — ask user to review the spec file before proceeding
9. **Transition to implementation** — invoke writing-plans skill to create implementation plan

## Process Flow

```dot
digraph brainstorming {
    "Explore project context" [shape=box];
    "Visual questions ahead?" [shape=diamond];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [shape=box];
    "Ask clarifying questions" [shape=box];
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" [shape=box];
    "Present design sections" [shape=box];
    "User approves design?" [shape=diamond];
    "Write design doc" [shape=box];
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" [shape=box];
    "User reviews spec?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke writing-plans skill" [shape=doublecircle];

    "Explore project context" -> "Visual questions ahead?";
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" [label="yes"];
    "Visual questions ahead?" -> "Ask clarifying questions" [label="no"];
    "Offer Visual Companion\n(own message, no other content)" -> "Ask clarifying questions";
    "Ask clarifying questions" -> "Propose 2-3 approaches";
    "Propose 2-3 approaches" -> "Present design sections";
    "Present design sections" -> "User approves design?";
    "User approves design?" -> "Present design sections" [label="no, revise"];
    "User approves design?" -> "Write design doc" [label="yes"];
    "Write design doc" -> "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)";
    "Spec self-review\n(fix inline)" -> "User reviews spec?";
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Write design doc" [label="changes requested"];
    "User reviews spec?" -> "Invoke writing-plans skill" [label="approved"];
}
```

**The terminal state is invoking writing-plans.** Do NOT invoke frontend-design, mcp-builder, or any other implementation skill. The ONLY skill you invoke after brainstorming is writing-plans.

## The Process

**Understanding the idea:**

- Check out the current project state first (files, docs, recent commits)
- Before asking detailed questions, assess scope: if the request describes multiple independent subsystems (e.g., "build a platform with chat, file storage, billing, and analytics"), flag this immediately. Don't spend questions refining details of a project that needs to be decomposed first.
- If the project is too large for a single spec, help the user decompose into sub-projects: what are the independent pieces, how do they relate, what order should they be built? Then brainstorm the first sub-project through the normal design flow. Each sub-project gets its own spec → plan → implementation cycle.
- For appropriately-scoped projects, ask questions one at a time to refine the idea
- Prefer multiple choice questions when possible, but open-ended is fine too
- Only one question per message - if a topic needs more exploration, break it into multiple questions
- Focus on understanding: purpose, constraints, success criteria

**Exploring approaches:**

- Propose 2-3 different approaches with trade-offs
- Present options conversationally with your recommendation and reasoning
- Lead with your recommended option and explain why

**Presenting the design:**

- Once you believe you understand what you're building, present the design
- Scale each section to its complexity: a few sentences if straightforward, up to 200-300 words if nuanced
- Ask after each section whether it looks right so far
- Cover: architecture, components, data flow, error handling, testing
- Be ready to go back and clarify if something doesn't make sense

**Design for isolation and clarity:**

- Break the system into smaller units that each have one clear purpose, communicate through well-defined interfaces, and can be understood and tested independently
- For each unit, you should be able to answer: what does it do, how do you use it, and what does it depend on?
- Can someone understand what a unit does without reading its internals? Can you change the internals without breaking consumers? If not, the boundaries need work.
- Smaller, well-bounded units are also easier for you to work with - you reason better about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more reliable when files are focused. When a file grows large, that's often a signal that it's doing too much.

**Working in existing codebases:**

- Explore the current structure before proposing changes. Follow existing patterns.
- Where existing code has problems that affect the work (e.g., a file that's grown too large, unclear boundaries, tangled responsibilities), include targeted improvements as part of the design - the way a good developer improves code they're working in.
- Don't propose unrelated refactoring. Stay focused on what serves the current goal.

## After the Design

**Documentation:**

- Write the validated design (spec) to `docs/superpowers/specs/YYYY-MM-DD-<topic>-design.md`
  - (User preferences for spec location override this default)
- Use elements-of-style:writing-clearly-and-concisely skill if available
- Commit the design document to git

**Spec Self-Review:**
After writing the spec document, look at it with fresh eyes:

1. **Placeholder scan:** Any "TBD", "TODO", incomplete sections, or vague requirements? Fix them.
2. **Internal consistency:** Do any sections contradict each other? Does the architecture match the feature descriptions?
3. **Scope check:** Is this focused enough for a single implementation plan, or does it need decomposition?
4. **Ambiguity check:** Could any requirement be interpreted two different ways? If so, pick one and make it explicit.

Fix any issues inline. No need to re-review — just fix and move on.

**User Review Gate:**
After the spec review loop passes, ask the user to review the written spec before proceeding:

> "Spec written and committed to `<path>`. Please review it and let me know if you want to make any changes before we start writing out the implementation plan."

Wait for the user's response. If they request changes, make them and re-run the spec review loop. Only proceed once the user approves.

**Implementation:**

- Invoke the writing-plans skill to create a detailed implementation plan
- Do NOT invoke any other skill. writing-plans is the next step.

## Key Principles

- **One question at a time** - Don't overwhelm with multiple questions
- **Multiple choice preferred** - Easier to answer than open-ended when possible
- **YAGNI ruthlessly** - Remove unnecessary features from all designs
- **Explore alternatives** - Always propose 2-3 approaches before settling
- **Incremental validation** - Present design, get approval before moving on
- **Be flexible** - Go back and clarify when something doesn't make sense

## Visual Companion

A browser-based companion for showing mockups, diagrams, and visual options during brainstorming. Available as a tool — not a mode. Accepting the companion means it's available for questions that benefit from visual treatment; it does NOT mean every question goes through the browser.

**Offering the companion:** When you anticipate that upcoming questions will involve visual content (mockups, layouts, diagrams), offer it once for consent:
> "Some of what we're working on might be easier to explain if I can show it to you in a web browser. I can put together mockups, diagrams, comparisons, and other visuals as we go. This feature is still new and can be token-intensive. Want to try it? (Requires opening a local URL)"

**This offer MUST be its own message.** Do not combine it with clarifying questions, context summaries, or any other content. The message should contain ONLY the offer above and nothing else. Wait for the user's response before continuing. If they decline, proceed with text-only brainstorming.

**Per-question decision:** Even after the user accepts, decide FOR EACH QUESTION whether to use the browser or the terminal. The test: **would the user understand this better by seeing it than reading it?**

- **Use the browser** for content that IS visual — mockups, wireframes, layout comparisons, architecture diagrams, side-by-side visual designs
- **Use the terminal** for content that is text — requirements questions, conceptual choices, tradeoff lists, A/B/C/D text options, scope decisions

A question about a UI topic is not automatically a visual question. "What does personality mean in this context?" is a conceptual question — use the terminal. "Which wizard layout works better?" is a visual question — use the browser.

If they agree to the companion, read the detailed guide before proceeding:
`skills/brainstorming/visual-companion.md`

name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence ≠ evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter ≠ compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion ≠ excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
✅ [Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
❌ "Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
✅ Write → Run (pass) → Revert fix → Run (MUST FAIL) → Restore → Run (pass)
❌ "I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
✅ [Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
❌ "Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
✅ Re-read plan → Create checklist → Verify each → Report gaps or completion
❌ "Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
✅ Agent reports success → Check VCS diff → Verify changes → Report actual state
❌ Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion → redirect → rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.
